error_text_too_long: TODO_TRANSLATE:Text too long — please shorten it.

argform_content_hint: TODO_TRANSLATE:Links are auto-detected. Just paste URLs into your text.
argform_content_placeholder: TODO_TRANSLATE:State your reasoning. Paste sources as URLs — they will be classified automatically.
argform_emotional_hint: TODO_TRANSLATE:(Herzensangelegenheit — a heart matter. Overrides evidence detection.)
argcol_add_disabled_support: TODO_TRANSLATE:Daily budget for support arguments depleted
argcol_add_disabled_reject: TODO_TRANSLATE:Daily budget for reject arguments depleted
my_page_subtitle: TODO_TRANSLATE:Theses you created or voted on.
my_empty_state: TODO_TRANSLATE:You haven't participated yet. Go vote or create a thesis!
my_standpoint_sub: TODO_TRANSLATE:Reflection report on your work so far — no blaming, just a reference.
my_budget_sub: TODO_TRANSLATE:What you've spent from today's budget (limit: {limit} per bucket).
pulse_page_subtitle: TODO_TRANSLATE:What moves the community — observed, not judged.
about_lead: TODO_TRANSLATE:Quappe is an argumentation platform. Theses, arguments, evidence — no comment wars, no flame trails, no anonymous insults masked as opinion.
about_purpose_p1_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Language is imprecise, opinions are everywhere — but commonality is hard to find. Social media has built bubbles, fronts and hate. Quappe is a different attempt: a structured space where positions can be stated clearly, supported with reasoning, challenged with rigour, and respected even where they differ.
about_purpose_p2_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:We search for shared ground. Where we disagree, we disagree with respect. Opinion without argumentation simply weighs less in the discourse — that is the design.
about_how_1_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Theses are the central entity — a claim someone proposes.
about_how_2_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Arguments support or reject a thesis. They can carry evidence.
about_how_3_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Fork instead of reply. Disagree with an argument? Copy it, adapt it, and let the crowd vote.
about_how_4_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Votes are free. Creating costs a small daily budget — to encourage thoughtfulness.
about_how_5_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Complexity slider controls how much you see at once.
about_how_6_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Updates feed (/my/updates) — see who forks your arguments, who reacts to your theses. Adjust your position when it matters.
about_lifecycle_intro: TODO_TRANSLATE:A thesis is never static. It goes through phases — driven by real activity, not by anyone's opinion of it. The state you see on each card tells you where a thesis stands in the discourse right now.
about_lc_seedling_desc: TODO_TRANSLATE:Fresh out of the ground. Fewer than 7 days old, gathering first reactions.
about_lc_discussed_desc: TODO_TRANSLATE:Healthy activity. People are engaging, but no clear direction yet.
about_lc_contested_desc: TODO_TRANSLATE:Active but polarised — support and reject are roughly balanced. The hard, interesting cases live here.
about_lc_crystallized_desc: TODO_TRANSLATE:A clear majority (≥ 65 %) backed by solid arguments. Something the community has worked through.
about_lc_faded_desc: TODO_TRANSLATE:Activity dropped without reaching consensus. Not deleted — still reachable, just quiet.
about_lc_dormant_desc: TODO_TRANSLATE:Untouched for 90+ days. Kept as a cold reference; can wake up when new activity arrives.
about_lc_note_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:This is intentional. The platform is designed to let noise fade and surface what crystallises — not everything deserves the same attention forever.
about_rule_1: TODO_TRANSLATE:The integrity of a being is untouchable. Always.
about_rule_2: TODO_TRANSLATE:Separate the person from the position. Always.
about_rule_3: TODO_TRANSLATE:Good faith. Assume it. Earn it.
about_rule_4: TODO_TRANSLATE:Minorities deserve a voice and a safe space.
about_rule_5: TODO_TRANSLATE:Trolls get marked — kindly first, firmly later.
about_rule_6: TODO_TRANSLATE:Qualify yourself. Bring reasoning, bring evidence, bring nuance.
about_not_p: TODO_TRANSLATE:Quappe is not a public square for the unwilling. It is not a fighting place — except inside the arguments themselves. It is not an exposure of the vulnerable. And it is not a substitute for thinking — it is a tool for it.
about_transparency_intro: TODO_TRANSLATE:A short honest note on what runs where — so you know what you're using.
about_transp_identity_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Identity. Anonymous but server-verified. On first visit the server mints a random UUID, signs it with an HMAC secret, and stores it in an httpOnly cookie. Every request is checked against that signature — request bodies can't forge someone else's identity. No accounts, no email, no tracking cookies. Clear your cookies and you're a new person.
about_transp_abuse_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Abuse guards. Server-side length caps (title 200, description 2000, argument 800 chars), a 32 KB body cap, and token-bucket rate limits per IP + user (10 writes/min, 30 votes/min, 60 reads/min). Only the author can edit or delete their own thesis or argument.
about_transp_data_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Data storage. Everything lives in the Node process's memory for now. Restart the server and the state is gone (seed data reloads). Phase 2 will bring real persistence.
about_transp_search_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Semantic search. Runs locally. The multilingual e5 embedding model (via @xenova/transformers) is downloaded on first server start and cached under .cache/. No embeddings ever leave your machine.
about_transp_llm_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Reflection reports (/my, /pulse). Generated by a local LLM via Ollama, if you have it running. Your theses, arguments and votes are sent only to http://127.0.0.1:11434 on your own machine. User content is wrapped in delimiter tags with an explicit "this is data, not instructions" system prompt to reduce prompt-injection surface. If Ollama is not running, the pages fall back to raw stats — no third-party LLM is ever contacted.
about_transp_updates_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Updates feed. /my/updates aggregates reactions to your content (forks, new arguments, votes) from the server-side data. The "last seen" timestamp lives in localStorage; the badge counts anything newer than that.
about_transp_budget_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:Daily budget. Enforced client-side, stored in localStorage. The Your day panel reconstructs today's actions from the server so you can see where the budget went.
about_transp_analytics_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:No analytics. No trackers, no third-party scripts, no fingerprinting. The only network traffic is between your browser and this Quappe server (and optionally your local Ollama).
about_license_p_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:The code is source-available under a non-commercial license (PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0).
about_license_no_1: TODO_TRANSLATE:Selling it or running it as a paid service
about_license_no_2: TODO_TRANSLATE:Commercial advantage inside a for-profit company
about_license_note_html: TODO_TRANSLATE:This is deliberately not OSI-approved open source. We want the code visible and improvable — but we do not want anyone monetising the same anti-social patterns we're trying to leave behind.
settings_id_hint: TODO_TRANSLATE:This is your unique identity. It's stored locally and never shared visibly.
settings_role_hint: TODO_TRANSLATE:In MVP mode, all users are admins.
settings_bounds_hint: TODO_TRANSLATE:Adjust the min and max limits of the slider. The slider itself stays 0-100 but is mapped to the range you configure here.
settings_categories_hint: TODO_TRANSLATE:Manage the available categories for theses. Add or remove as needed.
settings_admin_hint: TODO_TRANSLATE:Available in MVP for everyone. Will be role-gated later.
settings_about_hint: TODO_TRANSLATE:Quappe is an argumentation platform. State theses, provide arguments, and find commonality through structured discourse. No usernames. No comment wars. Just arguments.
top_limit_note: TODO_TRANSLATE:Adjust the complexity slider to see more theses.
updates_page_subtitle: TODO_TRANSLATE:How others react to your content — the basis for refining your arguments.
updates_empty_body: TODO_TRANSLATE:When others react to your theses or arguments, you'll see it here.
